name: White Sauce Variations - Information
recipeIngredient: Salt and white pepper, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
2. Gradually whisk in the flour until smooth.
3. Slowly pour in the milk while continuously stirring to avoid lumps.
4. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and coats the back of a spoon.
5. Remove from heat and season with salt and white pepper as desired.
Variations:
- Asparagus Sauce: Add 1/2 cup cooked or canned asparagus, cut into small pieces.
- Caper Sauce: Add 3 to 4 tablespoons chopped capers and 1 teaspoon lemon juice.
- Cheese Sauce: Add 1/2 to 1 cup grated American cheese and a dash of Worcestershire sauce or paprika (optional).
- Cheese Olive Sauce: Add 1/2 to 1 cup grated American cheese and 1/2 cup stuffed olives.
- Cheese Tomato Sauce: Use 1/2 cup tomato juice or strained tomatoes. Add 1/4 cup grated cheese.
- Crabmeat Sauce: Add flaked canned crab meat, seasoned with dry sherry wine if desired.
- Cream Onion or Celery Sauce: Saute finely chopped onion or celery in butter. Add to the sauce.
- Creole Sauce: Use tomato juice or strained tomatoes. Saute minced onions, green pepper, and celery in butter before flour is added.
- Curry Sauce: Add 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon curry powder with dry ingredients.
- Dill Sauce: Add 3 tablespoons minced fresh dill.
- Egg Sauce: Add 2 chopped hard-cooked eggs, additional salt, and a dash of paprika.
- Goldenrod Sauce: Add 2 chopped egg whites. Pour over fish and sprinkle with chopped egg yolks.
- Green Pea Sauce: Add hot cooked or canned peas and chopped pimento.
- Horseradish Sauce: Add prepared horseradish and mustard to the sauce.
- Lobster or Shrimp Sauce: Add canned or cooked lobster meat or shrimp, cut into small pieces. Add dry white wine for flavor.
- Milanese Sauce: Add grated Parmesan cheese after thickening. Serve with veal.
- Mock Hollandaise Sauce: Mix equal parts mayonnaise and lemon juice.
- Mushroom Sauce: Add sliced mushrooms to the sauce.
- Mustard Sauce: Add prepared mustard to the sauce.
- Oyster Sauce: Gently cook small oysters in their liquid until plump, then add to the sauce.
- Paprika Sauce: Add paprika with dry ingredients. Season with lemon juice if desired.
- Pimento Sauce: Add chopped pimento and green pepper to the sauce.
